Resolves #10368

Refactored `AGENTS.md` §15.1 and §15.3 to accurately reflect the tool hierarchy established in §2.1. This change removes legacy instructions that incorrectly promoted `query_documents` as the primary conceptual tool, replacing them with a strict mandate to use the `ask_knowledge_base` embedded RAG sub-agent for all "understanding the how" queries. `query_documents` is now explicitly demoted to a secondary path-discovery role.

Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session 27016011-8ae9-48bb-af87-9479dd5b0fd0.

## Comments

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Approved

> **Peer-Review Opening:** Clean follow-up that resolves a real self-contradiction inside the freshly-merged AGENTS.md restructure (PR #10366). The §15.1 + §15.3 wording now aligns directly with the §2.1 Tool Hierarchy table — `ask_knowledge_base` correctly framed as primary, `query_documents` demoted to file-discovery secondary. The fix is small, surgical, and exactly the right shape for a copy-edit-class inconsistency.

> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
>

> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (per guide §7.1):** The new §15.1 frames `ask_knowledge_base` and `query_documents` as "two primary tools" — accurate within the Knowledge Base scope of §15, but the broader §2.1 Tool Hierarchy actually enumerates **four** priorities (`ask_knowledge_base` → `query_documents` → `view_file` → `query_raw_memories`). A reader landing directly on §15 (e.g., via cross-ref or context-pruning) might not realize the broader hierarchy exists. **Non-blocking nit:** consider adding a one-line cross-ref to §2.1 at the top of §15.1 (e.g., *"See also §2.1 for the full four-tier Anti-Hallucination tool hierarchy."*). Closes the doc-navigation loop without expanding the scope of this PR.
> 
> A second related observation: the word *"only"* in *"Use this **only** when you need exhaustive path enumeration"* is slightly stronger than the §2.1 framing, which describes `query_documents` more broadly as "File discovery / path lookup." Filtered searches like `type='ticket'` are also valid `query_documents` uses. Could soften to *"Use primarily when..."* in a follow-up if it surfaces empirically. Also non-blocking.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> - **`[KB_GAP]`**: Ticket #10368 has zero labels — `ai`, `enhancement`, `documentation` not applied per `.agent/skills/ticket-create/` mandate (`ai` label is **MANDATORY** for agent-created tickets). Discipline lapse during ticket creation, not the PR's fault. Worth a follow-up label-add via `manage_issue_labels` after merge.
> - **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: None observed.
> - **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Documentation-migration discipline lesson worth codifying. PR #10366 commit 2 moved AGENTS_STARTUP §3.1 → AGENTS.md §15.1 *verbatim*. Verbatim migration was the right correctness floor — no behavioral semantic drift introduced — but it preserved a pre-existing inversion that lived dormant in AGENTS_STARTUP.md (where §2.1 didn't sit alongside §3.1) and became visible only after migration co-located the two contradicting passages. **Codify in `pull-request §1` Stepping-Back protocol or as an explicit AGENTS.md restructure check:** when migrating doc content between files, re-read both source AND destination for cross-consistency with neighboring sections that didn't move. The conflict-surfacing-on-co-location pattern is recurrent.
>
